title: Rebase, Sequencer, Autosquash, and Topology Preservation · History Defense
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for rebase, sequencer, autosquash, and topology preservation and requires the smallest safe rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model rebase as commit selection, temporary reset, ordered replay, new object creation, and ref movement rather than branch movement magic.
Use --onto, upstream, branch, fork-point, and explicit revision sets to select exactly which commits move and where.
Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, exec, drop, break, label, reset, and merge actions while preserving dependency order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Case G83 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity. The learner must predict range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries, run git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD, explain four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review, test this changed condition: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge, and preserve this recovery boundary: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for protect the old tip, inspect rewritten mapping, compare patch series and trees with range-diff, test changed behavior, and recover from abort or error.

Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Gita Rebase Equivalence Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G90 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity. The learner must predict range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries, run git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD, explain four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review, test this changed condition: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge, and preserve this recovery boundary: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Protect the old tip, inspect rewritten mapping, compare patch series and trees with range-diff, test changed behavior, and recover from abort or error.
